title: What to know about Rheum rhabarbarum before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Rheum rhabarbarum is a medicinal herb that has been prized for its numerous health benefits, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of various gastrointestinal disorders. As a versatile and easy-to-grow crop, Rheum rhabarbarum is cultivated for its edible stalks, which are harvested in the early summer months and can be consumed fresh or dried for later use. Botanically, Rheum rhabarbarum belongs to the Rheum genus and is a perennial plant that can grow up to three feet in height, characterized by its large, ovate leaves and purple flowers. With a long history of use in traditional medicine, Rheum rhabarbarum has been referenced in ancient texts, such as those written by the Greek physician Dioscorides, who noted its use for a variety of health conditions.
